title: cephalotaxus alpina
intro: Cephalotaxus alpina, also known as Chinese plum-yew, is a species of evergreen coniferous plant native to China. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including containing podophyllotoxin, a lignan that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antitumor effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-viral properties. In herbal preparations, Cephalotaxus alpina is often used in the form of dried leaves, stems, and roots, which can be decocted in water to make a tea or infused in oil to create a topical remedy.
